items.final_wording: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.

source.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease
source.population: Adults and children at risk of or living with CKD, with age, cause, GFR, albuminuria, progression, treatment, and referral qualifiers.
source.setting: CKD detection, evaluation, monitoring, medication stewardship, referral, and longitudinal kidney care.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected CKD, GFR, albuminuria, medication-review, and referral documentation. Diagnosis, treatment, medicine changes or doses, fixed monitoring intervals beyond stated qualifiers, and UAE service pathways remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_location.heading: Monitoring GFR and albuminuria in CKD
exact_location.locator: Practice Points 2.1.1–2.1.5; summary PDF pages 40 and 81–82

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.

items.rationale: The KDIGO section supports clinician review of kidney function and albuminuria over time and documented change or progression context without automatically classifying CKD or cardiovascular risk.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-led cardiovascular risk review and documented lifestyle discussion without autonomously generating advice.

source.title: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
source.population: Adults receiving cardiovascular risk assessment or lipid-modification review for primary or secondary prevention.
source.setting: Primary care and other clinician-led cardiovascular prevention services.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult cardiovascular risk and lipid-modification assessment and monitoring; international only and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.3.12 — cardiovascular risk and lifestyle review

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-led cardiovascular risk review and documented lifestyle discussion without autonomously generating advice.

items.rationale: The NICE section supports clinician-led cardiovascular-risk review that includes kidney disease and patient preferences without generating a risk score or treatment.

items.final_wording: Supports periodic medication review, accurate medication lists, adherence, continued indication, interaction, monitoring, reconciliation, and nephrotoxic-medicine context without treatment changes or doses.

source.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease
source.population: Adults and children at risk of or living with CKD, with age, cause, GFR, albuminuria, progression, treatment, and referral qualifiers.
source.setting: CKD detection, evaluation, monitoring, medication stewardship, referral, and longitudinal kidney care.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected CKD, GFR, albuminuria, medication-review, and referral documentation. Diagnosis, treatment, medicine changes or doses, fixed monitoring intervals beyond stated qualifiers, and UAE service pathways remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_location.heading: Polypharmacy and drug stewardship
exact_location.locator: Practice Point 4.3.1 and Figure 46; summary PDF pages 50 and 135–137

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports periodic medication review, accurate medication lists, adherence, continued indication, interaction, monitoring, reconciliation, and nephrotoxic-medicine context without treatment changes or doses.

items.rationale: The KDIGO stewardship section was used only to support documenting clinician review of medicine-related kidney context; no medicine, dose, hold, or substitution is generated.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-led referral documentation for qualified sustained GFR decline, significant albuminuria with haematuria, refractory hypertension, persistent potassium abnormalities, and other listed circumstances.

source.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease
source.population: Adults and children at risk of or living with CKD, with age, cause, GFR, albuminuria, progression, treatment, and referral qualifiers.

source.exclusions: Exact for selected CKD, GFR, albuminuria, medication-review, and referral documentation. Diagnosis, treatment, medicine changes or doses, fixed monitoring intervals beyond stated qualifiers, and UAE service pathways remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_location.heading: Referral to specialist kidney care services
exact_location.locator: Practice Point 5.1.1 and Figure 48; summary PDF pages 51 and 140–141

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-led referral documentation for qualified sustained GFR decline, significant albuminuria with haematuria, refractory hypertension, persistent potassium abnormalities, and other listed circumstances.

items.rationale: Kidney referral criteria were reviewed for clinician context, but no referral is generated from the generic cardiac-risk workflow.
